compiled_state.title: Write failing integration tests for LINK/UNLINK event processing in bridge-outbound
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Write a comprehensive set of failing integration tests that define the expected behavior for LINK and UNLINK event processing in bridge-outbound.py. These tests serve as the executable specification for subsequent implementation stories. All tests MUST fail (RED) since the implementation does not yet exist.

## Test Cases Required

### LINK event tests:
1. LINK event with relation="relates_to" → creates Jira Relates link via set_relationship
2. Name-based link type validation: get_issue_link_types() confirms "Relates" exists
3. Link type caching: get_issue_link_types() called once per sync run
4. Pre-create dedup: existing Relates link between same endpoints → skip creation
5. Reciprocal dedup: A→B and B→A LINK events → only one Jira link created
6. Graceful degradation: Jira rejection → bridge_alert written, sync continues
7. Missing source SYNC → skip event
8. Missing target SYNC → bridge_alert, skip
9. Link type not found → bridge_alert with available types, skip
10. Non-relates_to LINK events → filtered out (only relates_to processed)

### UNLINK event tests:
11. UNLINK event → read-before-delete: get_issue_links, match both endpoints, delete specific link
12. Both-endpoint matching: wrong target → no delete
13. 404 on source issue → "already gone", no error
14. Jira rejection on delete → bridge_alert, continues
15. Concurrent modification (404/409 on delete) → "already gone"
16. Non-relates_to UNLINK events → filtered out
17. Missing source/target SYNC → skip

### Dedup and degradation:
18. Duplicate LINK events for same (source, target, relates_to) → no duplicate Jira links
19. Multiple Jira failures → each writes bridge_alert, none blocks others

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py (Edit: add integration test class/functions)

## Test Approach
Given: Mock AcliClient with configurable responses for get_issue_link_types(), get_issue_links(), set_relationship(), delete_issue_link(). Mock SYNC files in temp ticket directories.
When: process_outbound() called with crafted LINK/UNLINK events
Then: Assert correct AcliClient method calls, bridge_alert files, and continuation behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file contains LINK event processing tests
  Verify: grep -c "def test.*link" tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py | awk '{exit ($1 < 10)}'
- [ ] Test file contains UNLINK event processing tests
  Verify: grep -c "def test.*unlink" tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py | awk '{exit ($1 < 7)}'
- [ ] All new tests fail (RED state)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "link or unlink" --tb=short -q 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED\|ERROR"
- [ ] No existing tests broken
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/scripts/test_bridge_outbound.py -k "not link and not unlink" --tb=short -q
- [ ] Record failing test run output in story comment
  Verify: true
